labels.plain: Sign off on an action before it runs.
labels.technical: Bind an approval to the exact action + input hash.

labels.plain: Open the step-by-step view of what happened.
labels.technical: Open the Cinema replay / proof inspector for the workflow.

labels.plain: Re-run exactly what happened and confirm it reproduces.
labels.technical: Deterministic re-execution from the replay capsule.

labels.plain: Send someone a self-contained story they can verify on their own.
labels.technical: Export a portable .infrixstory.json proof story bundle.

labels.plain: Check a proof yourself, without trusting the node that made it.
labels.technical: Run the independent verifier over the portable evidence bundle.

labels.plain: The maths binding every step together checks out.
labels.technical: All cryptographic bindings (hashes, signatures, anchor consistency) verified.

labels.plain: A selective-disclosure proof checked out — the hidden facts are backed without revealing them.
labels.technical: Selective-disclosure proof verified against its commitment.

labels.plain: The confirmations came from genuinely separate operators, not one party.
labels.technical: Valid witnesses span the required number of DISTINCT independent operators.

labels.plain: Confirmed against Accumulate L0 right now — durable beyond Infrix.
labels.technical: Proof level L4: bundle anchor confirmed through a live L0 lookup.

labels.plain: Checked on this machine only — not yet confirmed against the chain.
labels.technical: Structurally consistent; no live L0 confirmation. Caps below L4.

labels.plain: You don't have to trust the Infrix node — the proof stands on its own.
labels.technical: Verdict reproducible from the portable bundle without trusting the producing node.

labels.plain: Every cryptographic link checks out without any network — no node trust needed.
labels.technical: Cryptographic bindings recomputed offline; L0 anchor not independently confirmed (caps at L3).

labels.plain: An operator vouches for this. It is their word, not a verified proof.
labels.technical: Operator attestation / configuration; NOT a cryptographic or L0 verification.

labels.plain: This release's evidence was checked and matches the exact code that shipped.
labels.technical: Release evidence bundle verified: signer custody + commit binding + manifest integrity.

labels.plain: We re-ran exactly what happened and got the same result.
labels.technical: Deterministic replay from the capsule reproduced the recorded outcome.

labels.plain: Independent parties confirmed the same event.
labels.technical: A threshold of independent witness receipts attests the bundle reproduces.

labels.plain: The approval token's validity window has passed.
The approval token's signature does not verify against the session that minted it.

labels.short: The approval does not match this input
labels.plain: The approval token is bound to a different action or a different input hash than the one submitted.
The session's approval mode requires a human to approve this write before it runs.

labels.short: This action is outside the session's capabilities
labels.plain: The session was not granted the capability to run this action.
Running the AGENTS.md golden path in a temp workspace did not produce the documented artifacts, or AGENTS.md cites an infrix command that no longer resolves.

labels.short: A dry-run is required before this action
labels.plain: This action mutates state and must be dry-run before it can be executed.
The input does not satisfy the action's input schema (a required field is missing or has the wrong type).

labels.short: Mainnet writes are blocked for agents
labels.plain: This action would write to mainnet, which the agent path blocks by default.

labels.short: This action is disabled under the current profile
labels.plain: The action's supported profiles do not include the session's profile.

labels.short: Release evidence must verify before publish
labels.plain: The release evidence did not verify, so it cannot be published.
The session's time-to-live has elapsed.

labels.short: The agent session was not found
labels.plain: No session with that id exists (it may have been revoked).
The action requires a signer, but the session does not permit signing.

labels.short: That agent action does not exist
labels.plain: No action with that id is registered in the Agent Action Protocol.

labels.short: The session's write budget is exhausted
labels.plain: The session has already performed the maximum number of writes it was granted.

labels.short: That flag or command isn't recognized
labels.plain: Infrix did not recognize a flag, argument, or subcommand in the command line.

labels.short: No release evidence proves the current commit
labels.plain: No release-evidence package proves HEAD — none was found, or the only packages present are historical/stale.

labels.short: The release evidence proves an older commit
labels.plain: The release-evidence package proves a commit other than the one being claimed (HEAD).
The human task-success study has fewer completed sessions, or a lower success rate, than the release threshold requires.
Infrix could not fetch the anchor transaction from Accumulate L0.

labels.short: The Accumulate L0 endpoint is unreachable
labels.plain: Infrix could not reach the configured Accumulate L0 endpoint.

labels.short: Timed out waiting for L0 finality
labels.plain: The anchor did not confirm on Accumulate L0 within the timeout.

labels.short: The signing key is not on the key page
labels.plain: The key that signed the request is not an authorized key on the declared L0 key page.

labels.short: The signer's key page was not found on L0
labels.plain: Accumulate L0 has no key page at the signer's declared URL.

labels.short: This node cannot verify the signer against Accumulate L0
labels.plain: This node cannot verify the signer against Accumulate L0.
labels.technical:
error_code: L0_KEY_PAGE_RESOLVER_MISSING
cause: the L0 key-page resolver is not configured on this node

labels.short: The signer key-page version is stale
labels.plain: The submitted signer version does not match the current key-page version on L0.

labels.short: The endpoint is the wrong network
labels.plain: The configured endpoint does not match the expected Accumulate network.
A release-grade launch-check cannot start because required inputs are absent (evidence package, node URL, tenant token, or L0 endpoint).

labels.short: The local phase ran past its time budget
labels.plain: A launch-check local-phase check (usually the full `go test ./...`) exceeded its phase timeout. This is never reported as a pass and never as a bare process kill.

labels.short: The recovered address does not match MetaMask
labels.plain: The address recovered from the signature does not match the connected MetaMask account.

labels.short: The key is not authorized on the L0 key page
labels.plain: MetaMask signed correctly, but the key is not a current authorized key on the declared Accumulate key page.
No EIP-1193 wallet provider (window.ethereum) is available.

labels.short: Could not recover the public key from the signature
labels.plain: The secp256k1 public key could not be recovered from the MetaMask signature.
The wallet provider does not support eth_signTypedData_v4.

labels.short: The signature was rejected in MetaMask
labels.plain: The user declined the connection or the signing prompt in MetaMask.

labels.short: The config's network does not match the claim
labels.plain: The config's bound network (its name, contents, and NETWORK_POSTURE_MANIFEST entry) disagrees with --claim-network — e.g. a mainnet config used for a Kermit claim — or a template/unmanifested config was used where a network-bound one is required.

labels.short: An off-domain network call was denied
labels.plain: The consumption trust gate refused a network call because the host is not the configured, allow-listed node host (or a redirect tried to leave it).

labels.short: A live L0 endpoint is required for this profile
labels.plain: The selected profile requires a reachable, cryptographically-verified L0 anchor, but none is configured.

labels.short: A profile requirement is not met
labels.plain: One or more load-bearing substrates do not meet the selected deployment profile.

labels.short: State mirror is required for this profile
labels.plain: The selected profile requires the L0 state mirror, but it is not enabled.

labels.short: TLS is required for this profile
labels.plain: The selected production profile requires the API to serve over TLS, but TLS is not enabled.

labels.short: L0 confirmation is unavailable for this proof
labels.plain: The verifier could not confirm the anchor against Accumulate L0.

labels.short: The proof claims more than it backs
labels.plain: A receipt or claim asserts a higher assurance than the evidence supports (e.g. L4 without an L0 anchor).
The portable evidence package could not be parsed or is structurally invalid.
A proof / evidence package did not pass verification (a check failed or the package is not what it claims).
The valid witnesses do not span enough distinct independent operators.
The bundle carries no replay capsule, so deterministic re-execution cannot run.

labels.short: The required witness quorum is missing
labels.plain: Not enough independent witness receipts attest this proof.
A wallet/provider (e.g. MetaMask) acceptance bundle is absent, or it does not bind to the claimed commit.

labels.short: The publication receipt does not bind to current evidence
labels.plain: The publication receipt proves an older commit or a different tarball hash than the current evidence.

labels.short: The receipt claims more than it backs
labels.plain: A shareable receipt asserts a higher assurance than its evidence supports (e.g. claims L4 with no live-L0 confirmation).
The requested balance could not be read (gas/IU accounting not available).
A required field of the governed result (plan, outcome, or evidence) is missing.

labels.short: The governed result has no outcome
labels.plain: The intent was submitted but no outcome record was produced.

labels.short: A proof was requested but is unavailable
labels.plain: The flow could not export a portable proof for this result.
Infrix needs the Go toolchain to build and run, but `go` was not found.

labels.short: Go is older than go.mod requires
labels.plain: Your Go toolchain is older than the version go.mod declares.
The TypeScript SDK or tooling needs its npm dependencies installed.

labels.short: The port is already in use
labels.plain: Another process is already listening on the port Infrix wants to bind.
Authoring WASM contracts needs the wasm32-unknown-unknown Rust target.
The exported trust-center JSON proves an older commit, or no current trust-center JSON exists where one is required.
Infrix hit an error it could not translate into specific guidance.

labels.short: The UX matrix exceeded its time budget
labels.plain: A UX matrix profile ran longer than its duration budget (release is budget-locked and never allows an override).

labels.short: The UX matrix is missing required coverage
labels.plain: A UX matrix run did not cover every required (group, viewport) for its profile.

labels.short: The UX matrix selector or summary is invalid
labels.plain: An unknown profile/route/viewport selector, or a malformed UX matrix summary (wrong schema or missing fields).

labels.short: The UX demo server port is in use
labels.plain: The UX matrix runner failed before any test ran because the demo server port is already in use.
The base chain Infrix anchors to.
labels.technical: Accumulate's layer-0 identity + settlement substrate; the neutral chain Infrix anchors evidence digests to and confirms them against.

labels.plain: The record on the base chain that pins your evidence in time.
labels.technical: An L0 transaction committing an evidence digest; confirming it raises the proof level to L4.

labels.plain: How strongly a result is backed.
labels.technical: The proof level (L0..L4) and governance level (ungoverned/G0..G2) an evidence bundle achieves; L4 requires a confirmed live L0 anchor.

labels.plain: The internal record structure Infrix uses to make workflows replayable.
labels.technical: The Intent -> Plan -> Approval -> Execution -> Outcome -> Evidence -> Anchor chain every governed operation flows through.

labels.plain: A way to prove a hidden fact without revealing it.
labels.technical: A selective-disclosure proof verified against a commitment, so a fact is backed without exposing the underlying data.

labels.plain: Genuinely separate parties, not one party wearing many hats.
labels.technical: Witnesses judged by the independent operator that runs them (via a witness registry), so a quorum reflects distinct operators, not distinct keys.

labels.plain: The list of keys allowed to act for an identity.
labels.technical: An Accumulate ADI key page: the on-chain set of authorized signing keys (and their version) Infrix checks a signer against.

labels.plain: How strong a posture a deployment promises to back.
labels.technical: A capability profile (local/kermit/public_production/full_public_production) whose claims are gated by substrate readiness.

labels.plain: A compact summary of what was verified.
labels.technical: The single canonical receipt (pkg/proofreceipt) every proof surface emits: outcome, assurance, trust answer, and the artifacts behind it.

labels.plain: A portable package that lets anyone re-run what happened.
labels.technical: The self-contained capsule carried inside a bundle (contract bytecode, state, recorded steps) enabling deterministic re-execution.

labels.plain: A load-bearing service Infrix depends on (like the chain it anchors to).
labels.technical: A pluggable backing service (L0 anchoring, state mirror, witness network, disclosure) whose readiness gates a deployment profile.

labels.plain: Independent parties confirming the same event.
labels.technical: A threshold of distinct, independent witness receipts that each re-verify a bundle; full_public_production requires a 2-of-3 distinct-operator quorum.

labels.plain: A development network endpoint you point Infrix at.
Accumulate's public test network — the only network used for pre-launch verification.
labels.technical: Accumulate Kermit testnet (NetworkName=DevNet); the canonical L0 endpoint for verification.

labels.plain: Everything runs on your machine — fast, deterministic, no network needed.
labels.technical: Local deterministic mode; no L0 endpoint, proofs cap below L4.

labels.plain: Accumulate's production network. Not used during pre-launch verification.
labels.technical: Accumulate mainnet L0; verification never targets mainnet pre-launch.

labels.plain: See which load-bearing services are ready and which are not.
Re-check this proof yourself without trusting the node.
Preview an action's effects before anything runs.
See exactly what was proved, what L0 proved, and what remains unverified.
Discover every action Infrix exposes, as structured JSON.
Open the step-by-step view of what happened.
Find what is misconfigured and the exact command to fix it.
Send someone a self-contained story they can verify themselves.
See a full governed flow end-to-end on your machine.
Confirm the anchor against Accumulate L0 to reach the strongest proof.
Public production plus an independent witness quorum.
labels.technical: full_public_production profile: public_production + 2-of-3 distinct-operator witness quorum.

labels.plain: Verified end-to-end against the public test network.
labels.technical: kermit profile: live L0 against Kermit testnet.

labels.plain: For trying things out on your machine.
labels.technical: local profile: no live L0, no durability guarantees.

labels.plain: The strongest posture: durable, independently verifiable, no node trust.
labels.technical: public_production profile: live L0 + state mirror + TLS + replay required.

labels.plain: Confirmed against the base chain Infrix anchors to — durable and neutral.
labels.technical: Anchor confirmed against Accumulate L0 (the neutral settlement substrate).

labels.plain: This answer comes from the Infrix node directly; an independent check has not been run.
labels.technical: Result asserted by the node; not independently re-verified.

labels.plain: Anyone can re-run the maths and reach the same answer — you don't have to trust Infrix.
labels.technical: Verdict is reproducible from the portable bundle without trusting the producing node.

labels.plain: An operator configured or attested this — it is not the same as a verified proof.
labels.technical: Operator attestation / configuration; not a cryptographic verification.

assuranceBadges.plain: The maths binding every step together checks out.
assuranceBadges.technical: All cryptographic bindings (hashes, signatures, anchor consistency) verified.
assuranceBadges.screenReader: Cryptographically verified: all cryptographic bindings checked.

assuranceBadges.plain: A selective-disclosure proof checked out — the hidden facts are backed without revealing them.
assuranceBadges.technical: Selective-disclosure proof verified against its commitment.
assuranceBadges.screenReader: Disclosure proof verified: a selective-disclosure proof checked out.

assuranceBadges.plain: The confirmations came from genuinely separate operators, not one party.
assuranceBadges.technical: Valid witnesses span the required number of DISTINCT independent operators.
assuranceBadges.screenReader: Distinct operators met: confirmations span separate independent operators.

assuranceBadges.plain: Confirmed against Accumulate L0 right now — durable beyond Infrix.
assuranceBadges.technical: Proof level L4: bundle anchor confirmed through a live L0 lookup.
assuranceBadges.screenReader: Live L0 verified: the anchor was confirmed against Accumulate L0.

assuranceBadges.plain: Checked on this machine only — not yet confirmed against the chain.
assuranceBadges.technical: Structurally consistent; no live L0 confirmation. Caps below L4.
assuranceBadges.screenReader: Local proof: checked on this machine only, not confirmed against Accumulate L0.

assuranceBadges.plain: You don't have to trust the Infrix node — the proof stands on its own.
assuranceBadges.technical: Verdict reproducible from the portable bundle without trusting the producing node.
assuranceBadges.screenReader: No node trust required: the proof is verifiable without trusting the node.

assuranceBadges.plain: Every cryptographic link checks out without any network — no node trust needed.
assuranceBadges.technical: Cryptographic bindings recomputed offline; L0 anchor not independently confirmed (caps at L3).
assuranceBadges.screenReader: Offline verified: cryptographic bindings checked offline; L0 not confirmed.

assuranceBadges.plain: An operator vouches for this. It is their word, not a verified proof.
assuranceBadges.technical: Operator attestation / configuration; NOT a cryptographic or L0 verification.
assuranceBadges.screenReader: Operator attested: vouched for by an operator, not independently verified.

assuranceBadges.plain: This release's evidence was checked and matches the exact code that shipped.
assuranceBadges.technical: Release evidence bundle verified: signer custody + commit binding + manifest integrity.
assuranceBadges.screenReader: Release evidence verified: the release evidence bundle checked out.

assuranceBadges.plain: We re-ran exactly what happened and got the same result.
assuranceBadges.technical: Deterministic replay from the capsule reproduced the recorded outcome.
assuranceBadges.screenReader: Replay matched: re-execution reproduced the recorded outcome.

assuranceBadges.plain: Independent parties confirmed the same event.
assuranceBadges.technical: A threshold of independent witness receipts attests the bundle reproduces.
assuranceBadges.screenReader: Witness quorum met: independent witnesses confirmed the event.

glossary.plain: The base chain Infrix anchors to.
glossary.technical: Accumulate's layer-0 identity + settlement substrate; the neutral chain Infrix anchors evidence digests to and confirms them against.
glossary.firstUseReplacement: the base chain Infrix anchors to

glossary.plain: The record on the base chain that pins your evidence in time.
glossary.technical: An L0 transaction committing an evidence digest; confirming it raises the proof level to L4.
glossary.firstUseReplacement: the record on the base chain that pins your evidence

glossary.plain: How strongly a result is backed.
glossary.technical: The proof level (L0..L4) and governance level (ungoverned/G0..G2) an evidence bundle achieves; L4 requires a confirmed live L0 anchor.
glossary.firstUseReplacement: how strongly this result is backed

glossary.plain: The internal record structure Infrix uses to make workflows replayable.
glossary.technical: The Intent -> Plan -> Approval -> Execution -> Outcome -> Evidence -> Anchor chain every governed operation flows through.
glossary.firstUseReplacement: the internal record of what happened

glossary.plain: A way to prove a hidden fact without revealing it.
glossary.technical: A selective-disclosure proof verified against a commitment, so a fact is backed without exposing the underlying data.
glossary.firstUseReplacement: a proof of a hidden fact that reveals nothing extra

glossary.plain: Genuinely separate parties, not one party wearing many hats.
glossary.technical: Witnesses judged by the independent operator that runs them (via a witness registry), so a quorum reflects distinct operators, not distinct keys.

glossary.plain: The list of keys allowed to act for an identity.
glossary.technical: An Accumulate ADI key page: the on-chain set of authorized signing keys (and their version) Infrix checks a signer against.
glossary.firstUseReplacement: the list of keys allowed to act for an identity

glossary.plain: How strong a posture a deployment promises to back.
glossary.technical: A capability profile (local/kermit/public_production/full_public_production) whose claims are gated by substrate readiness.
glossary.firstUseReplacement: how strong a posture this deployment backs

glossary.plain: A compact summary of what was verified.
glossary.technical: The single canonical receipt (pkg/proofreceipt) every proof surface emits: outcome, assurance, trust answer, and the artifacts behind it.
glossary.firstUseReplacement: a compact summary of what was verified

glossary.plain: A portable package that lets anyone re-run what happened.
glossary.technical: The self-contained capsule carried inside a bundle (contract bytecode, state, recorded steps) enabling deterministic re-execution.
glossary.firstUseReplacement: a package that lets anyone re-run what happened

glossary.plain: A load-bearing service Infrix depends on (like the chain it anchors to).
glossary.technical: A pluggable backing service (L0 anchoring, state mirror, witness network, disclosure) whose readiness gates a deployment profile.

glossary.plain: Independent parties confirming the same event.
glossary.technical: A threshold of distinct, independent witness receipts that each re-verify a bundle; full_public_production requires a 2-of-3 distinct-operator quorum.
glossary.firstUseReplacement: independent parties confirming the same event

errors.title: The approval has expired
errors.plainMeaning: The approval token's validity window has passed.

fixes.label: Re-approve the action

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: The approval token is invalid
errors.plainMeaning: The approval token's signature does not verify against the session that minted it.

fixes.label: Re-approve through the session that will run the action

errors.title: The approval does not match this input
errors.plainMeaning: The approval token is bound to a different action or a different input hash than the one submitted.

fixes.label: Re-approve the exact input you intend to run

errors.title: This action needs explicit approval
errors.plainMeaning: The session's approval mode requires a human to approve this write before it runs.

fixes.label: Approve the dry-run's request, then run

errors.title: This action is outside the session's capabilities
errors.plainMeaning: The session was not granted the capability to run this action.

fixes.label: Create a session that grants the capability

errors.title: The agent-docs smoke gate failed
errors.plainMeaning: Running the AGENTS.md golden path in a temp workspace did not produce the documented artifacts, or AGENTS.md cites an infrix command that no longer resolves.

fixes.label: Run the executable agent-docs smoke gate
List the documented commands and resolution

errors.title: A dry-run is required before this action
errors.plainMeaning: This action mutates state and must be dry-run before it can be executed.

fixes.label: Dry-run the action first

errors.title: The action input is invalid
errors.plainMeaning: The input does not satisfy the action's input schema (a required field is missing or has the wrong type).

fixes.label: Inspect the action's schema

errors.title: Mainnet writes are blocked for agents
errors.plainMeaning: This action would write to mainnet, which the agent path blocks by default.

fixes.label: Use a testnet (Kermit) target instead

errors.title: This action is disabled under the current profile
errors.plainMeaning: The action's supported profiles do not include the session's profile.

fixes.label: Run under a supported profile

errors.title: Release evidence must verify before publish
errors.plainMeaning: The release evidence did not verify, so it cannot be published.

fixes.label: Verify the evidence first

errors.title: The agent session has expired
errors.plainMeaning: The session's time-to-live has elapsed.

fixes.label: Create a fresh session

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: The agent session was not found
errors.plainMeaning: No session with that id exists (it may have been revoked).

fixes.label: Create a new session

errors.title: This session may not sign
errors.plainMeaning: The action requires a signer, but the session does not permit signing.

fixes.label: Use a flow where the human wallet signs (e.g. MetaMask)

errors.title: That agent action does not exist
errors.plainMeaning: No action with that id is registered in the Agent Action Protocol.

fixes.label: List the available actions

errors.title: The session's write budget is exhausted
errors.plainMeaning: The session has already performed the maximum number of writes it was granted.

fixes.label: Create a session with a larger write budget

errors.title: That flag or command isn't recognized
errors.plainMeaning: Infrix did not recognize a flag, argument, or subcommand in the command line.

fixes.label: See the valid flags and subcommands

errors.title: No release evidence proves the current commit
errors.plainMeaning: No release-evidence package proves HEAD — none was found, or the only packages present are historical/stale.

fixes.label: Check the evidence directory hygiene
Generate evidence for the current commit

errors.title: The release evidence proves an older commit
errors.plainMeaning: The release-evidence package proves a commit other than the one being claimed (HEAD).

fixes.label: See which commit the evidence proves
Regenerate evidence for the current commit

errors.title: The human study is incomplete
errors.plainMeaning: The human task-success study has fewer completed sessions, or a lower success rate, than the release threshold requires.

fixes.label: Aggregate the study sessions and read the gap

errors.title: The L0 anchor is unavailable
errors.plainMeaning: Infrix could not fetch the anchor transaction from Accumulate L0.
errors.assuranceImpact: The anchor cannot be independently confirmed, so the proof caps at L3 — it does not claim L4.

fixes.label: Retry against a reachable L0 endpoint

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: The Accumulate L0 endpoint is unreachable
errors.plainMeaning: Infrix could not reach the configured Accumulate L0 endpoint.
errors.assuranceImpact: Without a reachable L0 endpoint this proof cannot claim live L0 verification (it caps at L3).

fixes.label: Run local deterministic mode
Check connectivity to Kermit

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: Timed out waiting for L0 finality
errors.plainMeaning: The anchor did not confirm on Accumulate L0 within the timeout.
errors.assuranceImpact: The proof has not yet reached L4; it is not failed, just not final.

fixes.label: Retry verification with a longer timeout

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: The signing key is not on the key page
errors.plainMeaning: The key that signed the request is not an authorized key on the declared L0 key page.

fixes.label: Bind the key to the ADI key page
Or submit without the L0 key-page check (offline)

errors.title: The signer's key page was not found on L0
errors.plainMeaning: Accumulate L0 has no key page at the signer's declared URL.

fixes.label: Check the signer key-page URL

errors.title: This node cannot verify the signer against Accumulate L0
errors.plainMeaning: This node cannot verify the signer against Accumulate L0.

fixes.label: Start the node with an L0 endpoint
Or use local demo mode

errors.technical:
error_code: L0_KEY_PAGE_RESOLVER_MISSING
cause: the L0 key-page resolver is not configured on this node

errors.title: The signer key-page version is stale
errors.plainMeaning: The submitted signer version does not match the current key-page version on L0.

fixes.label: Re-read the current key-page version and re-sign

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: The endpoint is the wrong network
errors.plainMeaning: The configured endpoint does not match the expected Accumulate network.

fixes.label: Use the Kermit testnet for verification

errors.title: Release launch-check inputs are missing
errors.plainMeaning: A release-grade launch-check cannot start because required inputs are absent (evidence package, node URL, tenant token, or L0 endpoint).

fixes.label: Supply the release inputs

errors.title: The local phase ran past its time budget
errors.plainMeaning: A launch-check local-phase check (usually the full `go test ./...`) exceeded its phase timeout. This is never reported as a pass and never as a bare process kill.

fixes.label: Run the fast local profile for quick feedback
Re-run the full local phase with streaming progress

errors.title: The recovered address does not match MetaMask
errors.plainMeaning: The address recovered from the signature does not match the connected MetaMask account.

fixes.label: Make sure the same account that connected also signs

errors.title: The key is not authorized on the L0 key page
errors.plainMeaning: MetaMask signed correctly, but the key is not a current authorized key on the declared Accumulate key page.

fixes.label: Bind the MetaMask key to the ADI key page
Or submit without the key-page check

errors.title: No MetaMask provider was found
errors.plainMeaning: No EIP-1193 wallet provider (window.ethereum) is available.

fixes.label: Install/unlock MetaMask in the browser
Or run the deterministic headless demo

errors.title: Could not recover the public key from the signature
errors.plainMeaning: The secp256k1 public key could not be recovered from the MetaMask signature.

fixes.label: Re-run the signing flow with a fresh challenge

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: Typed-data signing is not supported
errors.plainMeaning: The wallet provider does not support eth_signTypedData_v4.

fixes.label: Use a wallet that supports EIP-712 (e.g. MetaMask)

errors.title: The signature was rejected in MetaMask
errors.plainMeaning: The user declined the connection or the signing prompt in MetaMask.

fixes.label: Re-run and approve the MetaMask prompt

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: The config's network does not match the claim
errors.plainMeaning: The config's bound network (its name, contents, and NETWORK_POSTURE_MANIFEST entry) disagrees with --claim-network — e.g. a mainnet config used for a Kermit claim — or a template/unmanifested config was used where a network-bound one is required.

fixes.label: Use the config bound to the claimed network
Or correct --claim-network to match the config

errors.title: An off-domain network call was denied
errors.plainMeaning: The consumption trust gate refused a network call because the host is not the configured, allow-listed node host (or a redirect tried to leave it).

fixes.label: Check the configured node endpoint
Re-run against the configured node

errors.title: A live L0 endpoint is required for this profile
errors.plainMeaning: The selected profile requires a reachable, cryptographically-verified L0 anchor, but none is configured.
errors.assuranceImpact: The deployment cannot back a public-production assurance claim without live L0.

fixes.label: Configure a live L0 endpoint in the node config
Check readiness

errors.title: A profile requirement is not met
errors.plainMeaning: One or more load-bearing substrates do not meet the selected deployment profile.
errors.assuranceImpact: The deployment cannot advertise an assurance profile it does not back.

fixes.label: See exactly which substrates are missing

errors.title: State mirror is required for this profile
errors.plainMeaning: The selected profile requires the L0 state mirror, but it is not enabled.

fixes.label: Enable l0.stateMirrorEnabled in the node config
Re-validate the profile

errors.title: TLS is required for this profile
errors.plainMeaning: The selected production profile requires the API to serve over TLS, but TLS is not enabled.

fixes.label: Enable api.tlsEnabled in the node config
Re-validate the profile

errors.title: L0 confirmation is unavailable for this proof
errors.plainMeaning: The verifier could not confirm the anchor against Accumulate L0.
errors.assuranceImpact: The proof is cryptographically valid offline but caps at L3 — it does not claim L4.

fixes.label: Supply a reachable L0 endpoint

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: The proof claims more than it backs
errors.plainMeaning: A receipt or claim asserts a higher assurance than the evidence supports (e.g. L4 without an L0 anchor).
errors.assuranceImpact: Infrix refuses to present a proof that claims more assurance than it backs.

fixes.label: Confirm the anchor against live L0 before claiming L4

errors.title: The proof bundle is malformed
errors.plainMeaning: The portable evidence package could not be parsed or is structurally invalid.

fixes.label: Re-export the proof from the producing flow
Inspect it

errors.title: The proof did not verify
errors.plainMeaning: A proof / evidence package did not pass verification (a check failed or the package is not what it claims).

fixes.label: Re-verify and read the failing check
Verify a package against L0

errors.title: Witness operator diversity not met
errors.plainMeaning: The valid witnesses do not span enough distinct independent operators.
errors.assuranceImpact: The proof does not meet the distinct-operator assurance.

fixes.label: Supply a witness registry mapping witnesses to operators

errors.title: Deterministic replay is unavailable
errors.plainMeaning: The bundle carries no replay capsule, so deterministic re-execution cannot run.
errors.assuranceImpact: The cryptographic and L0 verdicts still hold; only the replay check is skipped.

fixes.label: Export with a replay capsule (public-production profile)

errors.title: The required witness quorum is missing
errors.plainMeaning: Not enough independent witness receipts attest this proof.
errors.assuranceImpact: The proof does not meet the required witness-quorum assurance.

fixes.label: Verify without requiring witnesses, or gather a quorum

errors.title: Wallet/provider acceptance evidence is missing
errors.plainMeaning: A wallet/provider (e.g. MetaMask) acceptance bundle is absent, or it does not bind to the claimed commit.

fixes.label: Verify the captured acceptance bundle

errors.title: The publication receipt does not bind to current evidence
errors.plainMeaning: The publication receipt proves an older commit or a different tarball hash than the current evidence.

fixes.label: Publish and re-verify the current package
Verify the publication binds to HEAD

errors.title: The receipt claims more than it backs
errors.plainMeaning: A shareable receipt asserts a higher assurance than its evidence supports (e.g. claims L4 with no live-L0 confirmation).

fixes.label: Re-verify the bundle and read the honest verdict
Confirm the anchor against live L0 before claiming L4

errors.title: The balance is unavailable
errors.plainMeaning: The requested balance could not be read (gas/IU accounting not available).

fixes.label: Read the account's IU balance from the v4 REST surface (GET /v4/...)

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: The governed result is incomplete
errors.plainMeaning: A required field of the governed result (plan, outcome, or evidence) is missing.

fixes.label: Inspect the intent's spine artifacts

errors.title: The governed result has no outcome
errors.plainMeaning: The intent was submitted but no outcome record was produced.

fixes.label: Wait for the outcome, or inspect the intent

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: A proof was requested but is unavailable
errors.plainMeaning: The flow could not export a portable proof for this result.

fixes.label: Re-run with proof export enabled

errors.title: Go is not installed
errors.plainMeaning: Infrix needs the Go toolchain to build and run, but `go` was not found.

fixes.label: Install Go (1.25.7+)
Verify your environment

errors.title: Go is older than go.mod requires
errors.plainMeaning: Your Go toolchain is older than the version go.mod declares.

fixes.label: Upgrade Go to match go.mod
Check the exact required version

errors.title: Node dependencies are not installed
errors.plainMeaning: The TypeScript SDK or tooling needs its npm dependencies installed.

fixes.label: Install the SDK dependencies

errors.title: The port is already in use
errors.plainMeaning: Another process is already listening on the port Infrix wants to bind.

fixes.label: Stop a leftover demo
Use another port

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: Rust wasm32 target is missing
errors.plainMeaning: Authoring WASM contracts needs the wasm32-unknown-unknown Rust target.

fixes.label: Add the wasm32 target

errors.title: The trust-center facts are stale
errors.plainMeaning: The exported trust-center JSON proves an older commit, or no current trust-center JSON exists where one is required.

fixes.label: Re-export trust-center facts from current evidence

errors.title: Something went wrong
errors.plainMeaning: Infrix hit an error it could not translate into specific guidance.

fixes.label: Diagnose your environment

errors.title: The UX matrix exceeded its time budget
errors.plainMeaning: A UX matrix profile ran longer than its duration budget (release is budget-locked and never allows an override).

fixes.label: Read the budget verdict in the summary

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

errors.title: The UX matrix is missing required coverage
errors.plainMeaning: A UX matrix run did not cover every required (group, viewport) for its profile.

fixes.label: Run the full release matrix

errors.title: The UX matrix selector or summary is invalid
errors.plainMeaning: An unknown profile/route/viewport selector, or a malformed UX matrix summary (wrong schema or missing fields).

fixes.label: Run a known-good profile

errors.title: The UX demo server port is in use
errors.plainMeaning: The UX matrix runner failed before any test ran because the demo server port is already in use.

fixes.label: Stop a leftover demo server

errors.retryGuidance: This is often transient — wait a moment and try again.

nextActions.label: Check deployment posture
nextActions.plain: See which load-bearing services are ready and which are not.

nextActions.label: Copy the verifier command
nextActions.plain: Re-check this proof yourself without trusting the node.

nextActions.label: Dry-run an action
nextActions.plain: Preview an action's effects before anything runs.

nextActions.label: See the trust boundary
nextActions.plain: See exactly what was proved, what L0 proved, and what remains unverified.

nextActions.label: List agent actions
nextActions.plain: Discover every action Infrix exposes, as structured JSON.

nextActions.label: Watch the replay
nextActions.plain: Open the step-by-step view of what happened.

nextActions.label: Diagnose the environment
nextActions.plain: Find what is misconfigured and the exact command to fix it.

nextActions.label: Share a proof story
nextActions.plain: Send someone a self-contained story they can verify themselves.

nextActions.label: Run the demo
nextActions.plain: See a full governed flow end-to-end on your machine.

nextActions.label: Confirm against live L0
nextActions.plain: Confirm the anchor against Accumulate L0 to reach the strongest proof.

personas.title: Builder
personas.tagline: Ship something that works, see the outcome first.

personas.title: Operator
personas.tagline: Know the posture, find the failed substrate, get the fix.

personas.title: Auditor
personas.tagline: Copy the verifier command, check the proof yourself.

personas.title: Agent
personas.tagline: Consume the same status as structured JSON.
